creator: conradj@is.co.za
text:
Trying to boot an SMP kernel hangs half way. Tweaking the SMP settings
in the kernel has no effect. I believe the problem is due to the
unsupported Reliance chipset. I have tried 3.3-STABLE, 3.4-RELEASE and
4-20000127. The output of a non-SMP dmesg is below, note the pci not
being picked up correctly.

Fix: 

Dont use SMP and use a single processor only.

How-To-Repeat: 
Compile an SMP kernel direct from GENERIC with NAPIC=2(gives an error
otherwise saying set this to 2 from the default of 1).
